item.title: WhiteLotus: Walking Through a UEFI Bootkit
item.description: This one is a little bit cursed, I guess. Introduction In the previous blogs, I walked around the lower layers of the machine: the Reset Vector, the firmware path, SMM, and a bit of the strange world where the operating system has not fully taken control yet. WhiteLotus lives exactly in that foggy area. It is not a normal Windows program, and it is not a normal driver either. It runs before Windows becomes Windows.
